rulings.Case Title: MENESES, DIONISIO E. et al vs. DOMINGUEZ, MARY JEAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judgmt In Favor Of Pltf Againt All Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 16, 2010, line 18, PLAINTIFF DIONISIO E. MENESES, CECILLA MENESES, and, VANESSA LINDSAY, Motion For Judgment in Favor of Plaintiff Againt All Defendants. Off Calendar. As the court previously ruled on 1/26/2010, it lacks jurisdiction to entertain this motion. The Court entered a dismissal on 8/21/2002 and that dismissal has not been vacated. See Viego Bancorp, Inc. vs. Wood (1989) 217 Cal.App.3d 200, 206.=(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA HIRSCH VS. THE 2999 CALIFORNIA STREET HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 16, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT JANUS CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained without leave to amend intentional infliction of emotional distress claim, outrageous intentional conduct not pled with sufficient particularity. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JAY SHUMAN VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 16, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. First and sixth causes of action are denied. Plaintiff raises triable issue of fact regarding failure to accommodate claim based on timing of notice re: plaintiff's ADD & demotion; third cause of action is denied, Plaintiff raises triable issue of fact regarding failure to accommodate claim based on timing of notice re: plaintiff's ADD & demotion; second cause of action, is granted, plaintiff fails to create triable issue of fact regarding nexus between disability and his June 2008 termination; fourth and fifth causes of action are granted. Plaintiff's claims fail on a matter of law; exclusive workers compensation remedy. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN VS. CHARLES ENGLISH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 16, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Plaintiff shifted burden. rulings.Case Title: STRUCTURA GENERAL CONTRACTOR, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. KENNETH EISEN MR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Remove Mechanic'S Lien
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 16, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS KENNETH EISEN, DEBORAH DACUMOS Motion to Remove Mechanic's Lien. Is granted, plaintiff fails to satisfy burden with documentary evidence; lien appears untimely. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 16, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer to fifth cause of action sustained with leave to amend for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Demurrer to fourth cause of action sustained for negligent infliction of emotional distress as conceded by Plaintiff. Demurrer overruled as to third cause of action for negligence. =(302/AJR)
